questions.question: What does Big O notation describe?
options: B) Upper-bound growth of time or space complexity as input size increases
questions.correctAnswer: B) Upper-bound growth of time or space complexity as input size increases

questions.question: When is binary search valid?
options: B) Only when the search space is ordered or monotonic
questions.correctAnswer: B) Only when the search space is ordered or monotonic

questions.question: What is the key idea behind divide and conquer?
options: B) Split a problem into smaller independent subproblems, solve them, then combine results
C) Use more memory to reduce code
D) Only solve half the problem
questions.correctAnswer: B) Split a problem into smaller independent subproblems, solve them, then combine results

questions.question: Why is merge sort guaranteed O(n log n)?
options: A) Because merging is constant time
B) It divides input into log n levels and processes all n elements per level
questions.correctAnswer: B) It divides input into log n levels and processes all n elements per level

questions.question: What makes quicksort worst-case O(n^2)?
options: B) Consistently poor pivot choices causing highly unbalanced partitions
D) Using recursion instead of loops
questions.correctAnswer: B) Consistently poor pivot choices causing highly unbalanced partitions

questions.question: What is the sliding window technique best suited for?
options: B) Problems over contiguous ranges where you expand/shrink a current window efficiently
questions.correctAnswer: B) Problems over contiguous ranges where you expand/shrink a current window efficiently

questions.question: Why does a hash map often reduce brute-force nested loops?
options: B) It gives near O(1) average lookup for previously seen values
C) It removes duplicates by default
questions.correctAnswer: B) It gives near O(1) average lookup for previously seen values

questions.question: What is recursion base case for?
options: B) Stopping the recursive process to avoid infinite calls
questions.correctAnswer: B) Stopping the recursive process to avoid infinite calls

questions.question: What distinguishes backtracking from plain recursion?
options: B) Backtracking explores choices, then undoes state to try other possibilities
C) Backtracking only works on trees
questions.correctAnswer: B) Backtracking explores choices, then undoes state to try other possibilities

questions.question: When is BFS preferred over DFS for shortest path?
options: A) In weighted graphs with negative edges
B) In unweighted graphs when minimum number of edges matters
questions.correctAnswer: B) In unweighted graphs when minimum number of edges matters

questions.question: Why does Dijkstra's algorithm fail with negative edge weights?
options: A) It cannot use a priority queue
B) A node finalized early may later get a cheaper path through a negative edge
C) It requires directed graphs only
D) It only works on trees
questions.correctAnswer: B) A node finalized early may later get a cheaper path through a negative edge

questions.question: What problem does topological sorting solve?
options: B) Ordering DAG nodes so every directed edge goes from earlier to later
questions.correctAnswer: B) Ordering DAG nodes so every directed edge goes from earlier to later

questions.question: What is dynamic programming primarily about?
options: A) Sorting faster than O(n log n)
B) Solving overlapping subproblems and storing results to avoid recomputation
questions.correctAnswer: B) Solving overlapping subproblems and storing results to avoid recomputation

questions.question: What is the difference between memoization and tabulation?
options: B) Memoization is top-down cached recursion; tabulation is bottom-up iterative table filling
questions.correctAnswer: B) Memoization is top-down cached recursion; tabulation is bottom-up iterative table filling

questions.question: What makes a greedy algorithm valid?
options: A) It always runs in O(n)
B) The problem must have a property where locally optimal choices lead to a global optimum
C) It uses no extra space
D) It can always replace DP
questions.correctAnswer: B) The problem must have a property where locally optimal choices lead to a global optimum

questions.question: What does a min-heap guarantee?
options: B) The smallest element is at the root, with heap-order property maintained
C) Fast O(1) search for any arbitrary value
questions.correctAnswer: B) The smallest element is at the root, with heap-order property maintained

questions.question: Why is Union-Find effective for connectivity problems?
options: B) It supports near-constant-time union and find with path compression and union by rank
C) It stores all paths explicitly
questions.correctAnswer: B) It supports near-constant-time union and find with path compression and union by rank

questions.question: What does an invariant mean in algorithm design?
options: B) A condition that remains true during execution and helps prove correctness
questions.correctAnswer: B) A condition that remains true during execution and helps prove correctness

questions.question: Why are prefix sums useful?
options: B) They allow O(1) range-sum queries after O(n) preprocessing
questions.correctAnswer: B) They allow O(1) range-sum queries after O(n) preprocessing

questions.question: What is the main benefit of amortized analysis?
options: A) It gives exact best-case time
B) It bounds the average cost per operation over a sequence, even if some operations are expensive
questions.correctAnswer: B) It bounds the average cost per operation over a sequence, even if some operations are expensive

questions.question: What is the time complexity of binary search?
questions.correctAnswer: B) O(log n)

questions.question: Which algorithm is used for shortest path?
questions.correctAnswer: C) Dijkstra

questions.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of quicksort?
